div1.entryFree: Overblown (cf. O'erblow), blown away, having spent the power of blowing: is the storm o.? Tp. II, 2, 114. Hence == past, gone: to smile at scapes and perils o. Shr. V, 2, 3. this ague-fit of fear is o. R2 III, 2, 190. my choler being o. H6B I, 3, 155. domestic broils clean o. R3 II, 4, 61.
